XPhysicalAlloc

The XPhysicalAlloc function allocates a region of contiguous physical memory and returns a pointer to the newly allocated memory.

LPVOID XPhysicalAlloc(
  SIZE_T dwSize,                // size of region
  ULONG_PTR ulPhysicalAddress,  // base physical address
  ULONG_PTR ulAlignment,        // memory alignment
  DWORD flProtect               // memory protection and type
);

Parameters

dwSize
[in] Specifies the desired size, in bytes, of the region to be allocated. This value is rounded up to the nearest page size.
ulPhysicalAddress
[in] Specifies the desired starting address of the region to allocate. This value must be aligned on a page (4 KB) boundary. If ulPhysicalAddress is MAXULONG_PTR, the system determines where to allocate the region.
ulAlignment
[in] If ulPhysicalAddress is MAXULONG_PTR, then ulAlignment specifies the desired alignment for the starting address returned for the allocated memory. This parameter must be a power of two that is greater than or equal to the system page size (4 KB). Alternatively, if ulAlignment is zero, the default alignment (the system page size) will be used. If ulPhysicalAddress is not MAXULONG_PTR, then ulAlignment is ignored.

flProtect
[in] Specifies the access protection and optional memory type modifiers. One of the following access protection flags must be specified:
Value Meaning
PAGE_READONLY Enables read and execute access to the committed region of pages. An attempt to write to the committed region results in an access violation.
PAGE_READWRITE Enables read, write, and execute access to the committed region of pages.

In addition, one of the following flags may be specified to modify the characteristics of the allocated memory:

Value Meaning
PAGE_NOCACHE Allows no memory caching for memory in the committed region of pages. The hardware attributes for the physical memory will be treated as "no cache." This is useful for mapping a video frame buffer with no caching.
PAGE_WRITECOMBINE The allocated memory has a write-combining cache policy. This is useful for mapping a video frame buffer. Write combining is a special caching mode that batches writes to the same cache line so they can be transferred in a single bus clock. Write combining does not preserve ordering of the writes, a tradeoff that is usually acceptable for frame buffers.

Return Values

If the function succeeds, the return value is the base address of the allocated region of pages.

If the function fails, the return value is NULL. To get extended error information, call GetLastError.

Remarks

The XPhysicalAlloc function allocates contiguous physical memory and returns a pointer to the allocated memory. If there is not enough contiguous physical memory available to satisfy the request, the function will fail.

If MAXULONG_PTR is specified for ulPhysicalAddress, the system determines the address at which the physical memory will be allocated. The returned address will be aligned according to ulAlignment. For example, if ulAlignment is 0x2000, then ulPhysicalAddress will be aligned on an 8 KB boundary.

If a value other than MAXULONG_PTR is specified for ulPhysicalAddress, then the memory is allocated starting at the address specified in ulPhysicalAddress, and ulAlignment is ignored.

The allocated memory is not initialized to any known value. If zero-initialized memory is desired, the title must explicitly set the memory contents to zero.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Winbase.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xapilib.lib

See Also

Memory Management Functions, Xbox Development System Memory | XPhysicalFree, XPhysicalProtect, XQueryMemoryProtect
